rulings.Case Title: ROBERT G BAKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Moore Bros.¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff presented evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Baker was exposed to an asbestos-containing product (gloves and/or blankets) attributable to defendant. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEE HOLT VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pacific Coast Supply, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant¿s satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable fact whether Mr. Holt was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The statement in the reply memorandum on the preference motion has been deemed an admission of the issue of plaintiffs¿ lack of evidence and inability to obtain evidence. The objections to the initial and supplemental declarations of Mr. Ay are sustained. =(220/HEK) DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST SUPPLY, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

rulings.Case Title: LEE HOLT VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Pacific Coast Building Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant¿s satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable fact whether Mr. Holt was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The statement in the reply memorandum on the preference motion has been deemed an admission of the issue of plaintiffs¿ lack of evidence and inability to obtain evidence. The objections to the initial and supplemental declarations of Mr. Ay are sustained. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID J. PALOMARES VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Towne Motor Company¿s demurrer to first amended complaint is, at plaintiff¿s option, either sustained with ten days leave to amend or continued to September 8 to enable plaintiff to file a supplemental opposition brief addressing defendant¿s arguments regarding Oddone. Defendant¿s objections to Ms. Gilson¿s declaration are sustained. Because defendant did not cite Oddone until its reply brief, it deprived plaintiff of an opportunity to address that case, which appears to be supportive of defendant¿s position that the first amended complaint does not state a cause of action against defendant. Plaintiff should advise defendant and the court of its choice by 4pm today via an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Wednesday, September 1, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Sid E. Parker Boiler MFG.
